data.title: Why Is Tower Semiconductor Stock Gaining Monday?
data.description:
The stock was up 4.22% at $209.01 in premarket trading Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

The companies said the devices are fabricated at Tower's U.S. facilities and designed and sold by Axiro, supporting a more resilient domestic supply chain.

The beamforming ICs are ramping to volume production and are ready for deployment in critical defense applications, including radar and satellite communications.

The companies added that the chips set a new performance benchmark over existing alternatives, which could help drive design wins and strengthen their positioning in secure foundry services for defense customers.

TSEM Technical Outlook: Momentum And Key Levels

Tower Semiconductor is trading within a strong technical setup, currently positioned at $209.01, which is 3.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 28.3% above its 50-day SMA. This suggests a bullish short-term trend, as the stock remains well above these key moving averages, indicating strong buying momentum.

The stock has shown impressive performance over the past year, with a 12-month return of 444.65%. This significant gain reflects robust long-term bullish sentiment among investors, positioning the stock favorably within its 52-week range, where it is currently closer to its high of $228.73.

  • Key Resistance: $228.73 — This level represents the recent high and a potential barrier for upward movement.
  • Key Support: $200.63 — This level is near the 20-day SMA, indicating where buying interest could emerge.

TSEM Earnings Date, Estimates And Analyst Ratings

Tower Semiconductor is slated to provide its next financial update on May 13, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: 55 cents (Up from 45 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $410.98 million (Up from $358.17 million)
  • Valuation: P/E of 103.4x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $161.75. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Benchmark: Buy (Raises Target to $230.00) (March 26)
  • Barclays: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $142.00) (February 13)
  • Wedbush: Neutral (Raises Target to $140.00) (February 12)

TSEM ETF Exposure: Invesco Semiconductors ETF (PSI)

  • Invesco Semiconductors ETF (NYSE:PSI): 4.07% Weight

Significance: Because TSEM carries meaningful weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

TSEM Stock Price Activity: Tower Semiconductor shares were up 4.22% at $209.01 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Why AEye Stock Is Trending Monday: New Leadership And Production Ramps
data.description:
• AEye stock is trending Monday. What should traders watch with LIDR?

What AEye’s Leadership Change Means for Growth

The company also approved an inducement grant of 125,000 restricted stock units, with one-third vesting in May 2027 and the rest vesting quarterly over the following two years. That structure signals AEye is paying to retain operations leadership through a multi-year production ramp.

Critical Price Levels for AEye Stock To Watch

AEye is sitting in the lower half of its 52-week range ($6.44 high, 50 cents low), which frames the current rebound as a recovery attempt rather than a return to prior peaks. The stock is trading 25.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 13.9% above its 100-day SMA, which points to improving short- and intermediate-term trend pressure even after a volatile year.

The moving average structure is still conflicted: the 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA, and the death cross that formed in February (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) is a reminder that longer-term trend damage hasn't fully cleared. At the same time, price is only 2.4% below the 200-day SMA, so a clean reclaim of that long-term line is the technical "line in the sand" many investors watch for trend repair.

Momentum is leaning constructive on the moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, with the MACD line above the signal line and a positive histogram, consistent with buyers having the near-term edge. In everyday terms, MACD being above its signal says upside momentum is currently stronger than downside pressure, even if it can still cool quickly.

  • Key Resistance: $2.50 — a level where rallies have recently tended to stall
  • Key Support: $2.00 — an area where buyers have shown up to defend pullbacks

What Is AEye And How Does It Operate?

AEye is a provider of high-performance, active lidar systems used in vehicle autonomy, driver-assistance systems and robotic vision. Its software-definable 4Sight Intelligent Sensing Platform combines solid-state active lidar, an optionally fused low-light HD camera and integrated deterministic AI to capture more useful information with less data.

That "scale into production" step is where operations and quality leadership matters, which helps explain why the market is reacting to an operations-focused executive hire. The company generates most of its revenue from the U.S., followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific, so manufacturing execution and supply chain consistency can directly affect how quickly it can convert programs into repeatable shipments.

AEye Earnings Preview: What To Expect In May

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13 (confirmed) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 11 cents (Up from Loss of 33 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: 28 cents million (Up from 6 cents million YoY)

LIDR Stock Price Movement in Premarket Trading

LIDR Stock Price Activity: AEye shares were trading at $2.19 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Courtesy of Aeye
